subject: Sports
title: Kathrine Switzer: The First Woman to Run Boston
description: A quick win: understand Kathrine Switzer: The First Woman to Run Boston.

hook.text: A race official tried to physically drag her off the course. She kept running. It was the 1967 Boston Marathon.

buildup.text: Kathrine Switzer registered as K.V. Switzer—hiding her gender because women were banned from running marathons.

discovery.text: Mid-race, official Jock Semple grabbed her bib and tried to remove her. Her boyfriend body-blocked him.

twist.text: She finished the race in 4:20. Photos of the confrontation went worldwide, sparking outrage and debate.

climax.text: Women were officially allowed to run the Boston Marathon in 1972. The women's Olympic marathon started in 1984.

punchline.text: They tried to drag her off the course. She ran 26.2 miles anyway.

quiz.question: What happened when Kathrine Switzer ran the 1967 Boston Marathon?
options: A race official tried to physically remove her from the course
She was cheered by the entire crowd
She was disqualified before the start
